records.citation: Zhang W, Wu H, Qin J. Domesticating SAM for Breast Ultrasound Image Segmentation via Spatial-Frequency Fusion and Uncertainty Correction. ECCV 2024, LNCS 15069: 20-37.
records.relevance: Uncertainty correction and separated false-positive/false-negative correction motivate module D.
records.distinction: UDER operates as an identity-initialized CNN refinement head and combines prediction entropy with multiscale disagreement; it is not SF-RecSAM or a SAM adaptation.

records.citation: Sun D, Dong C, Yan Y, et al. Challenge-aware U-net for breast lesion segmentation in ultrasound images. Pattern Recognition, 2025, 168: 111851.
records.relevance: Three ultrasound challenges, adaptive aggregation, and channel graph reasoning motivate modules A and B.
records.distinction: TriCAR inserts lightweight shared-backbone experts instead of three complete encoders; CGR-Bridge uses a bounded zero-initialized residual for parameter-compatible progressive training.

records.citation: Yang X, Zhang J, Ou Y, et al. Multilevel perception boundary-guided network for breast lesion segmentation in ultrasound images. Medical Physics, 2025, 52(5): 3117-3134.
records.relevance: Morphological boundary extraction and boundary-guided fusion support explicit geometry modeling in module C.
records.distinction: BD-CoRefine jointly predicts a boundary map and a signed distance field, then applies an identity-initialized geometry residual; PBNet uses MGPM, BGM, and boundary-enhanced loss.

records.citation: Pawlowska A, Karwat P, Zolek N. Letter to the Editor. Re: Dataset of breast ultrasound images by W. Al-Dhabyani, M. Gomaa, H. Khaled & A. Fahmy. Data in Brief, 2023, 48: 109247.
records.relevance: Supports explicit disclosure of BUSI duplicate and annotation-quality concerns.
records.distinction: The project additionally audits the fixed split 3 for exact and perceptually near-duplicate cross-split images.

integrity_note: Metadata was checked against publisher or PubMed pages. This file records provenance and design influence; it is not evidence that the proposed modules are globally novel.
